seoTitle: Mount Horner vs Mount Lawley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Horner and Mount Lawley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Horner vs Mount Lawley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Horner and Mount Lawley.
heading: Mount Horner vs Mount Lawley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Horner, Western Australia and Mount Lawley, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Lawley has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Mount Horner (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Horner or Mount Lawley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Horner has a flood risk score of 60/100, while Mount Lawley scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Horner scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Lawley scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Horner or Mount Lawley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Lawley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
